opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The real latency bottleneck in OS-integrated agents is typically tool invocation and state synchronization, not the LLM inference itself. When designing agents with persistent OS presence, I've found that context window management becomes critical—you need strict policies for what gets retained vs. dropped between sessions to prevent memory bloat. A common gotcha is that LLMs will hallucinate tool return values when the tool doesn't actually exist, so you need schema validation at the handler layer, not just in the prompt.**
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider LLM routing systems, tool orchestration frameworks, and self-improving agent loops that directly relate to agent architecture and execution.**
